title: Test suite sends real Telegram messages to Ash's phone (guest-approval notify path) + approval message renders literal <code> tags
source: Ash's phone, 2026-07-07 03:09 - '[Approval needed] alice wants to run run_script' arrived during a background verify.py run; screenshot in session
current_state: Two bugs, one incident. (1) agent/tools.py:317-328: when a guest profile requests a gated tool, the approval path best-effort notifies Ash via tools.tools_telegram.send_message, which reads TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID straight from .env at import time. testing/ had NO conftest.py and the approval tests (test_execution_approval.py / test_guest_gates.py, 'alice' fixtures) patch get_registry but not the Telegram send - so every verify.py run fired real approval messages at Ash's phone. (2) The message body hand-writes ... HTML, but send_message escapes its input before sending (its contract is unformatted text), so the tags render literally on the phone.
target_state: testing/conftest.py blanks TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID before any test module imports (pytest guarantees conftest-first; app/config.py's load_dotenv() never overrides existing env vars, so the blanks win over .env in every pytest subprocess verify.py spawns). The approval message contains no hand-written HTML. Acceptance: a full verify.py run completes with zero messages arriving on Ash's phone.
migration_plan: 1. Add testing/conftest.py setting TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN='' and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID='' via os.environ at module level.
2. Strip the <code> tags from the approval notify f-string in agent/tools.py (send_message escapes input itself).
3. python scripts/verify.py must print PASS (never pipe, T-214) AND Ash confirms no Telegram message arrived during the run.
risk_notes: The env blank makes _get_bot() return None so every real-send path degrades to {'success': False, ...} - all senders in the codebase treat that as best-effort already (try/except or ignored result). Tests that exercise Telegram behavior use fake bots or patch module functions and are unaffected. Do NOT 'fix' this by mocking send_message in the two approval tests only - any future test touching any best-effort send path would regress it; the env blank kills the class.
